summary:
• Iran's potential return to nuclear talks and currency crisis create uncertainty around Strait of Hormuz disruption risks that could significantly impact Western Hemisphere crude supplies despite current modest price reactions.

• Ukraine's escalating attacks on Russian Black Sea oil infrastructure and Syria's continued Russian oil dependence signal persistent geopolitical supply chain vulnerabilities in Eastern Europe that could redirect crude flows globally.

• Venezuela's seven-year export highs and new Canada-US pipeline capacity represent offsetting Western Hemisphere supply dynamics, though US production growth remains modest and constrains overall regional output expansion.
